resources.string: To run hermes-agent locally on Android, install Termux and Termux:API from F-Droid. Then return to Hermes to continue.
Or get it from GitHub Releases

Hermes needs four things from your Termux install before the local install can run. Do these in order, then tap Retry:

1. Install Termux and (recommended) Termux:API from F-Droid. Termux:API powers the battery / sensor / telephony features Hermes uses after install.

2. Grant the “Run commands inside Termux environment” permission: tap Open App Settings → Additional permissions → enable it.

3. In Termux, enable external apps: long-press the Termux screen → Properties → toggle “allow-external-apps” (or run `echo allow-external-apps=true >> ~/.termux/termux.properties` then `termux-reload-settings`).

4. Return to Hermes and tap Retry.
A few quick steps so Hermes can run inside Termux.
Hermes runs inside Termux on Android. Install Termux from F-Droid (the only official source) and return here.
Still not detected? Make sure the install finished and try again.
Hermes needs the “Run commands inside Termux environment” permission from Android. Tap Open App Settings → Additional permissions → enable it, then return here.
Command: writes `allow-external-apps=true` to `~/.termux/termux.properties` and reloads Termux settings.
We couldn’t read `allow-external-apps=true` from `~/.termux/termux.properties`. Go back to step 3 and try again.
Pick how to install Hermes inside Termux.
The in-app installer shows progress and lets you retry failed stages. The one-line installer is the canonical desktop path and is useful if our installer hits an edge case we don’t cover.
